subject: Accounting
title: Payroll Bookkeeping
description: A quick, practical guide to Payroll Bookkeeping.

hook.text: An employee earns $5,000 but takes home $3,800. The rest? Taxes, insurance, and retirement.

buildup.text: Payroll bookkeeping records gross wages, withholdings, employer taxes, and net pay for every employee.

discovery.text: It creates liabilities for taxes owed to the government and benefits owed to providers.

twist.text: Payroll errors are among the most common bookkeeping mistakes—and the most costly in penalties.

climax.text: Accurate payroll protects employees, satisfies the IRS, and keeps the business out of legal trouble.

punchline.text: Payroll has zero room for error. Get it right every time.

quiz.question: What does payroll bookkeeping track?
options: Gross wages, withholdings, employer taxes, and net pay
Just the company's total labor cost
